description: A type confusion vulnerability exists in V8's WebAssembly wasm-to-JS wrapper tier-up logic in Runtime_TierUpWasmToJSWrapper() (src/runtime/runtime-wasm.cc). The bug is triggered when a cross-module function table import is used: Module A imports a JS function, creates a function table with it as an element, and exports that table; Module B imports the table from A. When InitializeImportedIndirectFunctionTable() (src/wasm/module-instantiate.cc) creates the WasmImportData for Module B's dispatch table, it copies Module A's serialized FunctionSig via NewWasmImportData(orig_import_data) and sets the call origin to Module B's instance+table-index (SetCrossInstanceTableIndexAsCallOrigin). During tier-up, Runtime_TierUpWasmToJSWrapper() deserializes `sig` from import_data (carrying Module A's module-specific ValueType encodings, including type indices relative to Module A's type section), but resolves `trusted_data` and `module` from Module B (via the Tuple2 call origin). CompileWasmImportCallWrapper is then invoked with a CompilationEnv from Module B's NativeModule but a FunctionSig containing Module A's type indices. Because reference-typed parameters encode module-local type indices, the mismatch allows the wrapper to be miscompiled against the wrong type table, producing arbitrary WASM type confusion exploitable for memory corruption and sandbox bypass.

fixes.description: Backport/adaptation of CL 5921410 for the serialized-signature version in this vulnerable image. Test-only/status hunks omitted.
fixes.evidence_text: 5fcbf3954eb9f7f8221f068b5324e5b6f04b5839 [wasm] Don't tier up wrapper if signature depends on other instance
